LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-22-2018 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANT O'NEILL CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR A GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION OF ITS SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND ANN IANNUCCILLO. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. O'NEILL HAS SATISFIED ITS BURDEN TO PROVIDE THE INFORMATION REQUIRED FOR A GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION PER TECH-BILT AND CROSS-DEFENDANT TREANOR TILING, THE ONLY PARTY TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION, HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS EITHER OUTSIDE THE "BALLPARK" OR ANY OTHER REASON WARRANTING THE DENIAL OF THIS MOTION. TREANOR'S RELIANCE ON RADFORD FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT OCI MUST PROVIDE A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO TREANOR IS MISPLACED. UNLIKE WHAT OCCURRED IN RADFORD, TREANOR HAS BEEN PROVIDED THE MATERIAL TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT. CONTRARY TO TREANOR'S ASSERTION THAT IT WILL NOT RECEIVE ANY OFFSET PER CCP 877(A), OCI HAS ALLOCATED PORTIONS OF THE SETTLEMENT TO STEARMAN COSTS AND THE IANNUCILLOS' "OUT-OF-POCKET" COSTS, AS WELL AS VALUED ITS ASSIGNMENT OF ITS CROSS-CLAIMS. THE COURT IS NOT AWARE OF ANY AUTHORITY REQUIRING OCI TO PROVIDE A VALUATION FOR EACH OF THE TWO ASSIGNMENTS OF CROSS-CLAIMS NOR DOES THERE APPEAR TO BE A NEED TO DO SO IN THIS CASE. AS ARGUED BY OCI, THE VALUATIONS AND ALLOCATIONS MADE BY A SETTLING PARTY FOR A GOOD FAITH MOTION ARE NOT BINDING ON THE TRIAL COURT IF THE REMAINDER OF CASE PROCEEDS TO TRIAL. A PROPOSED ORDER WAS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. UDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of And Motion For Summary Judgment Of Treanor Tiling
Off Calendar Dec-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kris Cox, Moscone Emblidge & Otis LLP for Plaintiff, 415-362-3599
Terrence J. Brasch, Bishop Barry for Defendant Treanor Tiling, 510-596-0888

RULING - A R G U E D.

CROSS DEFENDANT TREANOR TILING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Treanor Tiling's motion for summary judgment is denied. Treanor's motion fails at the outset because it is premised on the assumption that the claims alleged in the second amended complaint are not implied contractual indemnity claims, yet liberally construing those claims, they are implied contractual indemnity claims. The economic loss doctrine does not apply to contractual claims. The joint obligation for an implied contractual indemnity claim can be based on the contractual obligation that a subcontractor owes to an owner. (See, e.g. Loduca v. Polyzos (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1334 (property owner is third party beneficiary of subcontract).) Accordingly, even without consideration of the disputed testimony of plaintiffs' expert, the motion must be denied due to Treanor's failure to satisfy its summary judgment motion burden.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Oneill Construction, Inc. For Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeff Loew, Esq. of Boornazian Jensen & Garthe, 510.834.4350, appearing for the Defendant O'Neill Construction, Inc.;
Terrence J. Brasch, Esq. of Bishop Barry, 510.596.0888, Defendant Treanor Tiling.

Matter called - Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant O'Neill Construction, Inc. For Good Faith Settlement Determination.

Ruling: ARGUED. AT the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant O'Neill Construction, Inc.'s motion for a good faith determination of its settlement with plaintiffs John and Ann Iannuccillo is granted. O'Neill has satisfied its burden to provide the information required for a good faith determination per Tech-Bilt and cross-defendant Treanor Tiling, the only party to oppose this motion, has not shown that the settlement is either outside the "ballpark" or any other reason warranting the denial of this motion. Treanor's reliance on Radford for the proposition that OCI must provide a copy of the settlement agreement to Treanor is misplaced. Unlike what occurred in Radford, Treanor has been provided the material terms of the settlement. Contrary to Treanor's assertion that it will not receive any offset per CCP 877(a), OCI has allocated portions of the settlement to Stearman costs and the Iannucillos' "out-of-pocket" costs, as well as valued its assignment of its cross-claims. The court is not aware of any authority requiring OCI to provide a valuation for each of the two assignments of cross-claims nor does there appear to be a need to do so in this case. As argued by OCI, the valuations and allocations made by a settling party for a good faith motion are not binding on the trial court if the remainder of case proceeds to trial. A proposed order was submitted to the Court.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
